frameworks.name: early vs late noise decomposition
frameworks.one_line: noise injected at value-coding versus choice-readout stages drives opposite context effects

frameworks.name: context-dependent choice
frameworks.one_line: contextual modulation of option values can help or hurt accuracy depending on noise locus

frameworks.name: value coding and decision-making models
frameworks.one_line: neural computations transforming option values into choices under stochastic perturbation

core_question: How do noise sources arising early versus late in the decision process differentially shape context-dependent choice?
key_assumptions: noise can be localized to distinct early (value-coding) and late (choice) stages
stochasticity in choice arises from internal noise sources
manipulations: induced value uncertainty to inject early noise
imposed time pressure to inject late noise
varied contextual information across option sets
key_findings: model simulations: early noise makes context enhance choice accuracy
model simulations: late noise makes context degrade choice accuracy
human experiments confirm dissociable positive and negative context effects
interpretations: opposing context effects reflect a unified mechanism gated by noise locus
reconciles prior conflicting findings on contextual influences in choice
